cards.definition: Small dams built to impound water during the monsoon, storing it for sprinkler irrigation in the dry months. They can also help rejuvenate wells that had gone dry.
cards.analogy: A savings account funded by the monsoon's surplus, withdrawn from in February and March when the plant needs it.
cards.why_it_matters: A capital-intensive structure compared to bunds or pits — worth noting whether it's a cooperative or individual-farm investment.
cards.cloze: ___ impound monsoon water for later sprinkler irrigation and can help rejuvenate wells that had gone dry.

cards.definition: Excavated storage — a pond or tank — that holds water collected during the monsoon for use later in the season.
cards.analogy: A bucket instead of a dam — smaller-scale, simpler storage doing the same basic job.
cards.why_it_matters: A more modest, lower-capital alternative to a check dam, worth distinguishing when a farmer mentions 'water storage' without specifying which kind.
cards.cloze: ___ are excavated storage that holds monsoon water for later use, a simpler alternative to a check dam.
